behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module-to-client handoff (taxonomy list must survive request construction and adapter serialization), so Unit is risky for this behavior wording. Real OS/network/subprocess is not required; an in-process stub client can catch missing/partial labels. Higher levels rejected: System is disqualified by behavior text focusing on request content, not real transport conditions; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because correctness does not depend on model output decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier module with the real request-builder/adapter stack and a stub API client that captures outbound payload. Invoke classification with a known taxonomy and assert every taxonomy label appears in the sent request exactly as expected.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This is an API-response-to-domain-output handoff across modules (client response parsing + taxonomy guard). A pure Unit test on a helper may miss wiring/parsing mismatches. Real infra is unnecessary; mocked/stubbed response is sufficient. Higher levels rejected: System is disqualified because behavior only requires handling a returned value; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because we can force a deterministic invalid label and do not rely on model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run classifier with real response parsing/normalization path and a stub provider that returns an out-of-taxonomy label (e.g., "medical"). Assert returned label is exactly "unknown".

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is explicitly about normalization determinism for identical input, which is a local function/object property with no required module interaction or I/O. Higher levels rejected: Integration is disqualified by behavior text narrowing scope to normalization step itself; System/Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because no real infra or model decision is needed when raw response is fixed.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization logic repeatedly with the exact same raw response fixture and taxonomy; assert identical output label each run.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires repeated live API calls and consistency under real model behavior. This depends on real AI output (non-determinism/drift), which mocks would hide. Workflow is rejected because this is repeated single-step classification calls, not a multi-step agent pipeline with sequential model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run a fixed hard-case canary set through live provider calls multiple times per case. Record raw provider responses and normalized labels; assert label changes are allowed only when raw provider response changed for that case.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at handoff between token-budget enforcement and dispatch path (truncate/reject must occur before send). In-process wiring is enough; no real network/OS needed. Higher levels rejected: System is disqualified because behavior is pre-dispatch enforcement; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because it does not depend on model output quality, only local policy application.
behaviors.test_description: Wire tokenizer/budget-policy/request-dispatch modules with a stub dispatcher. Feed over-budget input and assert policy outcome (truncate or reject) occurs and dispatch receives only compliant payload, or no dispatch on reject.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: This requires real provider-reported usage on live AI calls; mocked usage can mask true overages. Execution path depends on real model API behavior and returned usage metadata, so Integration is insufficient. Workflow rejected because this is a single-call measurement per case, not chained multi-agent decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Execute live classification calls on a fixed typical-document canary set using production token budget. Assert provider usage metadata for each call is <= configured budget; fail on any over-budget call.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is retry-orchestration between rate-limit signal handling, backoff, and retry invocation across local modules. A deterministic stub provider can reproduce this. Higher levels rejected: System is disqualified because real network timing is not required to validate logic; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because this behavior is infrastructure retry handling, not model-output correctness or multi-step agent reasoning.
behaviors.test_description: Use real retry/backoff orchestration with a stub provider returning rate-limit error first, success second. Assert module waits according to backoff policy (via controllable clock/sleeper) and returns successful label instead of surfacing immediate error.
